In my own life, I think if I could look at one element that really sets me apart from most other people and why I'm able to always succeed in that and what is thrown at me, it's this ability I have to get myself to do things I really do not feel like doing just because I know I have to do them. I don't want to, but I just get myself to do them day in and day out. And that comes down to my personal standard. See, in my mind, it would be an impossibility, a patent impossibility for me to go through life, let's say with this new program from the certification, and not deliver something at the highest level to almost fudge it, to not really apply myself, to say, you know what, good enough is good enough. And while I'm a big proponent of that, like in certain cases of taking action and just learning that you don't have to be an expert to start, you want to just be good enough sometimes and then become an expert, right? But when it comes to like a product you're making where you can say, I'll settle for good enough or I'll put an extra few days, an extra $5,000 to one particular item and see what that makes it excellent. See good enough is what? That's having a good standard. Having an excellent stand means you want to be excellent and that level above there, And again, you know, what I learned from Baylor, and I always cite Baylor when I go through the inner game, because I've really crystallized a lot of the things that I had already known because again, while I knew this stuff intuitively and it taught it to the Stratonites, right, and other people over the years, NLP was what sort of made it more formulaic for me because it raised my own awareness to what I was doing so effectively. In fact, as the story goes, when I went to see Baylor many years back, you know, when I was trying to dissect my own strategy, why was I able to take any salesperson and turn them into a world classical? I understood the straight line system. Yeah, that was powerful, right? That was a winning formula that allowed people to transfer certainty and close deals. But it was more than that. You know, I had these kids that would come to me and they were young, uneducated, disempowered, and they'd been told by their parents they were capable of greatness, any greatness they had intimated and beaten out of them, really. You know, so they were disempowered, average people with low standards, yet they walk into my boardroom and a few weeks later, they would look like different people, the way they walked, the way they talked, where they carried themselves, how they dressed, everything. And they say, well, okay, Jordan, I get that, but you know, you taught these guys to make millions of dollars a year. You gave me that much money. I would dress and look and act better. Even while that's true, it doesn't apply here because the Stratton training program was four months long. You get it? So when a kid came to me, he wasn't making money for four months, but the change was almost instant. Why? Because there were things I was saying to everybody about acting as if, raising your standard, leading with your body, leading with the way you speak. In other words, even though the money wasn't made yet, they already appeared to be wealthy. They were already acting, living, and not maybe spending the money, but they were applying, carrying themselves like winners. At the end of the day, that aspect, that inner game aspect, is what merges with the outer game and bam, creates this short-fire mess of success. And again, in the short term, the indicator is state management. Like, can you access your skills in the moment? I'm so glad about it because a daily motivation is a plane. I can keep going when I'm doing my real stuff. I got to stop playing. The point being is that while state management is your in-the-moment indicator for wealth and success, can you access the skills you have? Your standard is your long-term indicator, where you'll end up. If I stand, you end up in the right place. Low standard, you're not going to end up doing well in life. In the certification course, I really go through it in detail, ad-nord, how you raise your standard for all aspects of your life. But I've also gone through it less formally in some of the intensive. So if you have trouble with this, start there and you can get a real jump start on this. And then when I release certification, I'm sure you'll want that because it's ridiculously awesome. The point is, though, is our state is the in-the-moment indicator. Our standard is the long-term barometer that guides, dictates, and ensures our success.
